caption: Overview of the four major circulations: pulmonary, coronary, systemic, and portal.
alt: Anatomical schematic of the pulmonary, coronary, systemic, and portal circulations radiating from the heart and connecting to the lungs, body, and intestinal tract.

caption: The heart-forming region (HFR) within splanchnic mesoderm, showing the otic vesicle landmark.
alt: Embryonic disc with the heart-forming region marked between the otic vesicles and the third pair of somites in splanchnopleuric lateral plate mesoderm.

caption: Horseshoe-shaped intraembryonic coelom enclosing the cardiogenic area.
alt: Top-down view of the early embryo showing the U-shaped intraembryonic coelom enlarging cranially to form the pericardial cavity.

caption: Intraembryonic coelom before cephalic folding.
alt: Cross-section of the embryo prior to cephalic folding, showing the intraembryonic coelom flanked by lateral plate mesoderm.

caption: Pleuroperitoneal canal stage of intraembryonic coelom development.
alt: Embryonic cross-section labelling the pleuroperitoneal canal as the coelomic cavity expands cranially.

caption: Cardiogenic area moving into position after cephalic folding.
alt: Sagittal view after cephalic folding: the cardiogenic area sweeps ventrally into the future thoracic region.

caption: Isolated cavities appear in the lateral plate mesoderm that soon fuse to form the horse- (slide 7).

caption: Position of the heart tube after cephalic folding.
alt: Embryonic side view showing the heart tube relocated ventral to the foregut after cephalic folding.

caption: Fate of Intraembryonic Coelom (slide 9).
alt: Slide 9 diagram: Fate of Intraembryonic Coelom

caption: Molecular cascade activating NKX2.5 in splanchnic mesoderm.
alt: Schematic of anterior endoderm secreting BMP 2/4 and WNT inhibitors that activate the NKX2.5 transcription factor in the heart-forming splanchnic mesoderm.

caption: Paired endothelial strands forming the endocardial tubes.
alt: Embryonic cross-section showing two angioblastic blood islands coalescing into the paired endocardial heart tubes.

caption: Early formation in the form of paired endothelial strands -angioblastic blood islands t... (slide 11).
alt: Slide 11 diagram: Early formation in the form of paired endothelial strands -angioblastic blood islands t...

caption: Snapshot of heart development from straight tube to four-chambered organ.
alt: Composite figure tracing heart morphogenesis from primitive paired tubes through looping to the mature four-chambered heart.

caption: Fusion of the two endocardial tubes during lateral folding.
alt: Sequential cross-sections showing the two endocardial tubes approaching the midline and fusing into a single tube as lateral folding proceeds.

caption: VEGF-driven fusion of heart tubes in lateral folding.
alt: Diagram of lateral folding bringing the heart-forming regions together under VEGF signaling, fusing them into a single primitive heart tube.

caption: Cephalic folding suspends the heart by the dorsal mesocardium.
alt: Sagittal view of the heart tube suspended within the pericardial cavity by the dorsal mesocardium during cephalic folding.

caption: Dorsal mesocardium degenerating to form the transverse pericardial sinus.
alt: Cross-section showing breakdown of the dorsal mesocardium leaving the transverse pericardial sinus around the heart tube.

caption: Final cephalic-folding position of the early heart tube.
alt: Lateral view of the embryo with the heart tube settled into the pericardial cavity after cephalic folding completes.

caption: Lecture diagram (slide 15) illustrating heart tube formation and folding.
alt: Lecture slide 15 diagram from the Development of the Heart presentation, related to heart tube formation and folding.

caption: Three layers of the developing heart wall: endocardium, myocardium, epicardium.
alt: Cross-section through the heart wall labelling endocardium (innermost), myocardium with cardiac jelly, and epicardium derived from coelomic mesoderm.

caption: Cardinal, vitelline, and umbilical veins draining into the early heart.
alt: Schematic of the three paired venous systems — cardinal, vitelline, and umbilical — converging on the venous pole of the developing heart.

caption: Cardiac neural crest cell migration to the outflow tract.
alt: Diagram of neural crest cells from the upper somite region migrating through pharyngeal arches to populate the bulbar and truncal ridges.

caption: Formation of Primitive Chambers (slide 19).
alt: Slide 19 diagram: Formation of Primitive Chambers

caption: Formation of Primitive Chambers (slide 19).
alt: Slide 19 diagram: Formation of Primitive Chambers

caption: Bulboventricular looping bends the heart tube to the right.
alt: Three-stage diagram of the heart tube growing faster than the pericardial sac, looping rightward into the U-shaped bulboventricular loop.

caption: Final D-loop position of the bulboventricular loop.
alt: Lateral diagram of the heart in normal D-loop configuration with atria positioned dorsal to the ventricles.

caption: Mirror-image L-loop producing dextrocardia.
alt: Side-by-side comparison of normal D-loop versus abnormal L-loop, with the L-loop placing the cardiac apex on the right side.

caption: Dextrocardia with situs inversus contrasted with normal anatomy.
alt: Body schematic comparing situs solitus (heart apex left) with situs inversus totalis (heart apex right) following L-looping.

alt: Slide 24 diagram: Bulbo-ventricular looping

caption: Sinus venosus before incorporation into the right atrium.
alt: Cross-section showing the symmetric sinus venosus opening centrally into the dorsal wall of the primitive atrium.

caption: Hemodynamic shift driving sinoatrial orifice rightward.
alt: Schematic of left-to-right venous shunting that progressively enlarges the right horn of the sinus venosus.

caption: Adult right atrium derivatives of the right venous valve.
alt: Labelled diagram showing the cranial right venous valve becoming the crista terminalis and the caudal portion forming the valves of the IVC and coronary sinus.

caption: Lecture diagram (slide 30) illustrating septation and outflow tract development.
alt: Lecture slide 30 diagram from the Development of the Heart presentation, related to septation and outflow tract development.

caption: Sinus venosus shifting rightward to incorporate into the right atrium.
alt: Diagram tracing rightward enlargement of the right horn and regression of the left horn of the sinus venosus.

caption: Right and left venous valves at the sinoatrial orifice.
alt: Diagram of the right and left venous valves bracketing the sinoatrial opening as the sinus venosus joins the right atrium.

caption: Right Venous Valve of Sinu-atrial orifice (slide 32).
alt: Slide 32 diagram: Right Venous Valve of Sinu-atrial orifice

caption: Septum primum and septum secundum overlapping at the foramen ovale.
alt: Coronal diagram of the interatrial septa: septum primum as a thin flap valve and septum secundum forming the rigid superior wall.

caption: Left venous valve fused with the septum secundum.
alt: Detail of the left venous valve incorporating into the developing septum secundum on the right side of the interatrial septum.

caption: Coronary sinus and oblique vein of Marshall derivatives.
alt: Diagram showing the regressed left horn of the sinus venosus becoming the coronary sinus and the left common cardinal vein becoming the oblique vein of Marshall.

caption: Endocardial cushions partitioning the common AV canal.
alt: Frontal section of the AV canal with dorsal and ventral endocardial cushions fusing to create the right (tricuspid) and left (mitral) AV openings.

caption: Septum primum growing toward endocardial cushions, leaving foramen primum.
alt: Coronal diagram of the interatrial septum primum descending from the roof of the primordial atrium with the ostium primum below its free edge.

caption: Programmed cell death creating the foramen secundum.
alt: Sequential diagrams showing apoptotic perforations in the upper septum primum coalescing into the foramen secundum before the foramen primum closes.

caption: Septum secundum growing to the right of septum primum.
alt: Coronal diagram of the rigid septum secundum descending to the right of the thin septum primum, partially covering the foramen secundum.

caption: Septum primum acting as a flap valve over the foramen ovale.
alt: Cross-section showing fetal right-to-left shunting through the foramen ovale, with septum primum functioning as a one-way valve.

caption: Adult fossa ovalis from the fused septa.
alt: Postnatal diagram of the closed foramen ovale: septum primum forms the floor of the fossa ovalis and septum secundum forms the limbus.

caption: Atrial hemodynamics before and after birth.
alt: Side-by-side diagram contrasting prenatal right-to-left shunt across the foramen ovale with postnatal closure as left atrial pressure rises.

caption: Closure of the foramen ovale at birth.
alt: Diagram showing rising left atrial pressure pressing the septum primum flap against the septum secundum to close the foramen ovale.

caption: Primordial pulmonary vein incorporating into the smooth left atrium.
alt: Diagram showing the primordial pulmonary vein expanding into the dorsal wall of the left atrium to form its smooth-walled portion.

caption: Two parts of the interventricular septum: muscular and membranous.
alt: Coronal diagram of the IV septum showing the muscular component growing up from the floor and the membranous component descending from the AV cushions.

caption: Components fusing to form the complete interventricular septum.
alt: Exploded diagram of right and left bulbar ridges, dorsal endocardial cushion, membranous and muscular components combining into the final IV septum.

caption: Summary roadmap of interventricular septum formation.
alt: Schematic listing AV/CV canal alignment, AV partitioning, and IV septation as the sequential events that complete the interventricular septum.

caption: Bulbo-ventricular looping (slide 48).
alt: Slide 48 diagram: Bulbo-ventricular looping

caption: Key event 2 (slide 49).
alt: Slide 49 diagram: Key event 2

caption: AV cushion fusion separating tricuspid and mitral openings.
alt: Coronal diagram of dorsal and ventral AV cushions meeting in the midline to split the common AV canal.

caption: Primordial muscular IV septum growing toward the endocardial cushions.
alt: Lateral diagram of the muscular interventricular septum extending from the apex toward the AV cushions.

caption: Membranous septum closing the persistent IV gap.
alt: Coronal diagram of the membranous interventricular septum sealing the gap between the muscular IV septum and the bulbar ridges.

caption: Membranous IV septum fusing with the AP septum.
alt: Diagram showing the right side of the dorsal endocardial cushion fusing with the bulbar ridges to complete the membranous portion of the IV septum.

caption: Neural crest cells migrating into the outflow tract.
alt: Schematic of cardiac neural crest cells streaming through the pharyngeal arches into the bulbar and truncal ridges of the outflow tract.

caption: Lecture diagram (slide 53) illustrating septation and outflow tract development.
alt: Lecture slide 53 diagram from the Development of the Heart presentation, related to septation and outflow tract development.

caption: 180° spiraling of the aorticopulmonary septum.
alt: Three-stage diagram showing the bulbar and truncal ridges fusing and twisting 180° to align the aorta over the LV and pulmonary trunk over the RV.

caption: Final positioning of the great vessels after AP septation.
alt: End-stage diagram of the heart with the ascending aorta over the left ventricle and the pulmonary trunk over the right ventricle.

caption: Muscular IV septum forms (slide 56).
alt: Slide 56 diagram: Muscular IV septum forms

caption: Summary of IV septum formation: muscular, AP, and membranous parts.
alt: Composite summary diagram of the muscular IV septum forming first, the IV foramen persisting, and the AP septum closing it via the membranous component.

caption: Muscular IV septum forms (slide 56).
alt: Slide 56 diagram: Muscular IV septum forms

caption: Semilunar valve cusps forming around the great vessel orifices.
alt: Cross-section of three swellings of subendocardial tissue around the aorta and pulmonary trunk that develop into the semilunar valve cusps.

caption: Trabeculae carneae and papillary muscles forming via cavitation.
alt: Frontal view of the ventricular wall showing cavitation that produces the trabeculae carneae and papillary muscles supporting the AV valve cusps.
